turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been dealing with this crampy lower abdominal pain that's been getting worse over the past three weeks, and I'm really worried about what might be going on inside me.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The crampy lower abdominal pain is mostly on my left side, it comes and goes but it's been getting more frequent and intense over the past three weeks. Sometimes it feels like a sharp stabbing pain, other times it's more of a dull ache, but it's always in the same area.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 3 weeks ago. At first, I just had occasional cramps, but they've become more frequent and intense over time.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens pretty much every day now, sometimes several times a day.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's an 8 out of 10 in terms of severity - it really disrupts my daily activities and work.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: When I'm having these cramps, I usually feel a bit lightheaded and weak. It's definitely alarming me and making me worry about what might be going on inside my body.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: Actually, it seems to worsen after eating, especially if I've had something greasy or spicy. And I've also noticed that my symptoms seem to get a bit better when I'm able to relax and rest for a while.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: I do get a bit lightheaded and weak when I'm having these cramps, but I don't usually sweat or feel nauseous.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: Sometimes it radiates up to my lower back, but that's about it.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any swelling in my legs or feet.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been trying to manage the symptoms with loperamide (Imodium) for diarrhea, but it hasn't really provided much relief. As for other medications, I occasionally take ibuprofen 2-3 times a week.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: I don't have any diagnosed medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I've never had my cholesterol checked, and to be honest, I haven't been to a doctor in over 2 years.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I'm a non-smoker and I've been trying to manage stress from my symptoms and work by drinking more beer lately.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient:
My diet is pretty irregular due to long work hours, mostly takeout and convenience foods. As for exercise, I try to stay active but it's been a challenge with my symptoms - I've been feeling too fatigued to do much more than light stretching.

Also, I wanted to mention that I did have some bloody stools with mucus recently, which is really concerning me. The bright red blood mixed with stool has been coming out in the past week or so.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: My sister has Crohn's disease, diagnosed at 30 years old. That made me worry about possibly having something similar.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My sister has Crohn's disease and that's been on my mind a lot lately.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: I've been putting off seeing a doctor because I didn't want to deal with the hassle of going through tests and waiting for results. But now that things are getting worse, I'm really worried about what's going on inside me.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: The bloody stools with mucus are actually a pretty recent development, it started about a week ago and has been happening almost every day since then. It's this bright red blood mixed with stool that's really got me worried, I've never experienced anything like it before.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient:
I'm also experiencing some bloody stools with mucus, which is really concerning me. The bright red blood mixed with stool has been coming out in the past week or so, and it's definitely got me worried about what might be going on inside my body. It's almost every day now, and it's like nothing I've ever experienced before.

Also, I wanted to mention that I have some joint pain in my knees and ankles, it's been aching lately, but no swelling or redness. And I'm also feeling pretty fatigued, it's affecting my work performance and daily activities.

Oh, and one more thing, I've noticed some unintentional weight loss of about 4 kg (9 lbs) over the past three weeks, which is really worrying me.
